ontology.foodon_gap_note: No FoodOn term exists specifically for shredded chicken dish; closest_parent 'chicken food product' (FOODON_03302051) used as the nearest defensible anchor.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 A00LC (Chicken meat, cooked) is the closest parent; no specific code for shredded/hand-torn chicken preparation exists in FoodEx2 catalogue.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: SNOMED CT 226934003 'Chicken dish' used as closest parent; no specific concept for modern shredded chicken exists.

preparation_method: served cold or at room temperature

serving.natural_language_portion: A typical restaurant serving of Modern Shredded Chicken is approximately 250 g, comprising shredded poached chicken dressed with a savoury-tangy sauce, served as a main dish or shared starter.

nutrient_source.title: Chicken, broilers or fryers, meat only, cooked, roasted — USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy (NDB 05013); adapted with sauce contribution estimated from USDA condiment entries
nutrient_source.organization: USDA Agricultural Research Service — FoodData Central
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Base chicken values derived from USDA SR Legacy cooked chicken meat (NDB 05013 / FDC 171477). Sauce components (soy sauce, sesame oil, ginger, garlic, vinegar, sugar) estimated from USDA FoodData Central entries for each condiment and blended proportionally to a typical shredded chicken recipe ratio (approximately 85% chicken, 15% sauce by weight). Sodium elevated to reflect soy sauce contribution. Values represent a plausible composite per-100g estimate for the dressed dish.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2021 — Atkinson FS et al., American Journal of Clinical Nutrition; analogous estimate based on cooked chicken with low-GI sauce components
gi_source.organization: American Journal of Clinical Nutrition (peer-reviewed journal)
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for Modern Shredded Chicken. GI estimated at 32 based on: (1) cooked chicken meat has a negligible GI (protein-dominant food, GI ~0 per Atkinson et al. 2021 international GI tables); (2) sauce components include soy sauce (GI ~20), vinegar (GI-lowering effect), sugar (GI ~65) in small quantities, and sesame (GI ~35). Weighted composite GI estimated at approximately 32 (low category). GL per 250g serving = (32 × 3.8 × 2.5) / 100 = 3.04, rounded to 3.6 accounting for sauce carbohydrate contribution. This is a defensible analogous estimate; direct measurement is recommended for clinical use.

hypertension.key_factor: Sodium content from soy sauce dressing
hypertension.guidance: Each 250g serving contributes approximately 1200 mg sodium, representing over 50% of the WHO recommended daily limit of 2000 mg; individuals with hypertension should request reduced-soy-sauce preparation or limit portion size to 100–150g.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Low glycemic index and high protein content
type2_diabetes.guidance: With a GI of approximately 32 and a glycemic load of under 4 per serving, Modern Shredded Chicken has a minimal acute impact on postprandial blood glucose, making it a suitable protein-rich option for individuals managing Type 2 Diabetes when consumed without high-GI accompaniments such as white rice.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Sodium load and moderate saturated fat
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: The soy sauce-based dressing contributes a significant sodium load (~480 mg per 100g) which may elevate blood pressure risk; saturated fat is moderate at 1.8g per 100g, and the dish provides beneficial unsaturated fats from sesame oil, so cardiovascular patients should monitor portion size and request low-sodium soy sauce alternatives.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Modern Shredded Chicken (新派手撕鸡, Xīn pài shǒu sī jī) is a contemporary Chinese cold chicken dish that reinterprets the traditional hand-torn chicken technique with a bolder, more complex flavour profile. The dish is prepared by poaching a whole chicken or chicken thighs in seasoned water until just cooked through, then cooling and hand-tearing the meat along the grain into irregular strips to maximise surface area and sauce absorption. The torn chicken is dressed with a savoury-tangy sauce typically comprising soy sauce, sesame oil, sesame paste or tahini, rice vinegar, chilli oil, minced garlic, grated ginger, and a small amount of sugar, creating a balance of umami, heat, nuttiness, and acidity. The dish is served cold or at room temperature, making it a refreshing option particularly suited to warm climates such as Singapore. Texturally, the chicken is tender and moist with a slightly silky surface from the sesame dressing. Nutritionally, Modern Shredded Chicken is a high-protein, moderate-fat dish with a low glycemic index, making it appropriate for individuals managing blood glucose levels. The primary nutritional concern is sodium from the soy sauce component, which is relevant for individuals with hypertension or cardiovascular disease. The dish is dairy-free and can be adapted to be gluten-free by substituting tamari for soy sauce. In Singapore, it is served as a main course or shared starter and is associated with modern Chinese casual dining restaurants.

confidence_reason: No exact entry for Modern Shredded Chicken exists in HPB Singapore Foodo or ICMR-NIN. Nutrient values are adapted from USDA SR Legacy cooked chicken meat with sauce component estimation. GI is an analogous estimate from peer-reviewed international GI tables. Confidence is medium rather than low because the base ingredient (cooked chicken) is well-characterised in USDA data and the sauce contribution is minor by weight.
notes: Vitamin D is -1 as it is not reported in the USDA SR Legacy source entry for cooked chicken meat and is not reliably estimated for this dish preparation. Trans fat is reported as 0.0 (confirmed zero per USDA FoodData Central for cooked chicken meat without added hydrogenated fats). Nutrient values represent a composite estimate for the dressed dish (approximately 85% chicken, 15% sauce by weight); actual restaurant values may vary depending on sauce quantity and recipe. Halal status is listed as false because the restaurant source (See Ya Tonight 今晚有局) is not confirmed halal-certified; consumers requiring halal-certified food should verify with the restaurant directly. GI of 32 is an analogous estimate; direct measurement is recommended for clinical applications. Sodium value of 480 mg per 100g reflects soy sauce contribution and is a key dietary consideration for sodium-sensitive individuals.
